zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard於1225年出生在英國英格蘭肯特爾哈姆 卡素時，她的父親Baron Richard Fitzroy35歲，母親Rohese de Dover11222歲。她於1242年與Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley在英國英格蘭肯特爾哈姆 卡素結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1277年7月7日在英國格盧斯特斯爾恩波利享年52歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭布里斯托聖 奧古斯丁 馮 雷也 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard于1225年出生在英国英格兰肯特爾哈姆 卡素时，她的父亲Baron Richard Fitzroy35岁，母亲Rohese de Dover11222岁。她于1242年与Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley在英国英格兰肯特爾哈姆 卡素结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1277年7月7日在英国格盧斯特斯爾恩波利享年52岁，埋葬在英国英格兰布里斯托聖 奧古斯丁 馮 雷也 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Fitzroy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella

The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard was born in 1225, in Chilham Castle, Kent, England, United Kingdom, her father, Baron Richard Fitzroy, was 35 and her mother, Rohese de Dover, was 11222. She married Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley in 1242, in Chilham Castle, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 7 July 1277, in Thornbury, Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 52, and was buried in St Augustine The Less' Church, Bristol, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard est née en 1225, à Chilham Castle, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Baron Richard Fitzroy, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Rohese de Dover, avait 11222 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley en 1242, à Chilham Castle, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 7 juillet 1277, à Thornbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterrée à St Augustine The Less' Church, Bristol, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard 1225 in Chilham Castle, Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Baron Richard Fitzroy 35 und ihre Mutter Rohese de Dover 11222. Sie hat 1242 in Chilham Castle, Kent, England, United Kingdom Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 7. Juli 1277 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Thornbury, Gloucestershire, England und wurde in St Augustine The Less' Church, Bristol, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard è nata il nel 1225 nel Chilham Castle, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Baron Richard Fitzroy, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Rohese de Dover, aveva 11222 anni. Lei sposato Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley nel 1242, nel Chilham Castle, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 7 luglio 1277, nel Thornbury, Gloucestershire, England, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolta a St Augustine The Less' Church, Bristol, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichardが1225年にイギリスイギリスケントチルハム カストルで生まれた時，父Baron Richard Fitzroyは35歳，母Rohese de Doverは11222歳であった。1242年にイギリスイギリスケントチルハム カストルでSir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1277年7月7日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアソルンブリーで52歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスブリストルスト アウグスティン ス レス' チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.St. Augustine's Abbey, Bristol, Gloucest: 영국 잉글랜드 브리스톨 스트 오구스틴 테 레스' 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard(이)가 1225년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 치함 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Baron Richard Fitzroy(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Rohese de Dover(은)는 11222세였다. 그녀는 1242년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 치함 카스틀에서 Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1277년 7월 7일 영국 그루스스터실르 토른버이에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 브리스톨 스트 오구스틴 테 레스' 처르치에 묻혔다.

pt.Chilham Castle, Chilham, Kent, England: Chilham Castle, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.St. Augustine's Abbey, Bristol, Gloucest: St Augustine The Less' Church, Bristol, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard nasceu em 1225, em Chilham Castle, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Baron Richard Fitzroy, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Rohese de Dover, tinha 11222 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley em 1242, em Chilham Castle, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 7 de julho de 1277, em Thornbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, com 52 anos, e foi sepultada em St Augustine The Less' Church, Bristol, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.St. Augustine's Abbey, Bristol, Gloucest: ст аугустаин т лесс' чурч, бристол, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard родилась в 1225 г. в местности чилхам кастл, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Baron Richard Fitzroy было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Rohese de Dover, 11222. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley в 1242 г. в местности чилхам кастл, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 7 июля 1277 г. в местности таурнбури, глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 52 лет и была похоронена в местности ст аугустаин т лесс' чурч, бристол, Англия, Великобритания.

es.St. Augustine's Abbey, Bristol, Gloucest: St Augustine The Less' Church, Bristol, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard nació en 1225, en Chilham Castle, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Baron Richard Fitzroy, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Rohese de Dover, tenía 11222 años. Se casó con Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley en 1242, en Chilham Castle, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 7 de julio de 1277, en Thornbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultada en St Augustine The Less' Church, Bristol, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
